title: pimelea serpyllifolia
intro: Pimelea serpyllifolia, also known as the sticky currant, is a small shrub native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as its antioxidant properties that can help to protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Pimelea serpyllifolia include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against infections. In herbal preparations, Pimelea serpyllifolia is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
